title: Product Information API Schemas
description: JSON Schema definitions for the Siemens Product Information Webservice API

productNumber.description: The ID of the product (MLFB, EAN, ...).

link.description: Relative link to the product (using this API).

purchasabilityNote.description: Additional information on the product's purchasability that may provide guidance if no successor or substitute was found.

manufacturer.description: The company that makes the product.

salesRelease.description: Product can be ordered but not delivered. Existing dates and deadlines are not confirmed (PM280).

deliveryRelease.description: Product can be ordered and will be delivered from the shipping locations (PM300).

productCancellation.description: Product can still be ordered and delivered in the spare part business but incoming orders are rejected (PM410).

productDiscontinuation.description: Product can only be ordered and delivered in the spare part business in the context of warranty/repair (PM490).

supportUrl.description: URL of the product's support page.

purchasabilityStatus.description:
Information on how the product can be purchased.

PURCHASABLE = The product can still be purchased from the manufacturer.

SUBSTITUTE_PURCHASABLE = The original product can't be purchased anymore, but a substitute exists.

SUCCESSOR_PURCHASABLE = The original product can't be purchased anymore, but a successor exists.

NOT_PURCHASABLE = Neither the original product nor its successors or substitutes can be purchased.

obsolescenceLevel.description:
1 = Product has been discontinued and no successor or substitute exists.

2 = Item has been discontinued but a successor exists.

3 = Availability horizon of 1 year.

4 = Availability horizon of 1 to 2 years.

5 = Availability horizon of 2 to 5 years.

6 = Availability horizon of over 5 years.

successor.description:
The product's successor or `null` if no successor exists.
The successor is a product that is PARTIALLY compatible with the original.

substitute.description:
The product's substitute or `null` if no substitute exists.
The substitute is a product that is FULLY compatible with the original.

relatedProducts.description: Related products that may be used as successors or substitutes.

minimumOrderQuantity.description: The minimum order quantity for new parts.

countryOfOrigin.description: The country of production or manufacture, given as country-code.

commodityCode.description: The commodity code is a numerical code used to classify the product in international trade. It helps to facilitate trade, ensure the correct application of tariffs and taxes, and track the movement of goods for statistical purposes.

eccn.description: The Export Control Classification Number of the product.

deliveryTimes.description:
The standard lead time ex-works of the product.
Lead times vary for new, spare or repair parts.

A delivery time of `null` means that the product can only be delivered on request.

prices.description:
The prices of the product.
Prices vary for new, spare or repair parts.

customerId.description: The id of the customer (owner of the api key).

apiKeyExpiryDate.description: The expiry date of the api key, after which it can no longer be used.

credits.description: An object of numerical credits that this api key owns. Each credit belongs to a specific resource like `Obsolescence` or `Delivery` and is decremented for each request to that specific resource.
